subject: Ecology
title: Archaea in Soil
description: A micro-lesson that makes Archaea in Soil usable.

hook.text: Archaea look like bacteria under a microscope, but they belong to an entirely different domain.

buildup.text: For decades, scientists thought archaea lived only in extreme environments—hot springs, salt lakes.

discovery.text: We now know archaea are abundant in ordinary soil, driving key nitrogen and carbon cycles.

twist.text: Ammonia-oxidizing archaea convert ammonia to nitrite—a critical step in the nitrogen cycle.

climax.text: Archaea may outnumber bacteria in some soils, yet most species remain unnamed and unstudied.

punchline.text: The third domain of life hides in plain dirt.

quiz.question: What key process do ammonia-oxidizing archaea perform?
